title: Boat Rental in Siargao: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Siargao, the surfing capital of the Philippines, offers a gateway to the crystal-clear waters of the Philippine Sea. Renting a yacht here provides access to secluded islands and world-class waves; a half-day motorboat charter from General Luna costs around 250 EUR. BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified local operators for your yacht charter Siargao experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Siargao?

A shared boat tour starts at 40 EUR per person, while a private motorboat for half a day costs from 250 EUR. Prices vary by boat size and season. A full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 guests averages 600 EUR. Crewed yacht charters begin at 1,200 EUR per day, including fuel and crew. These rates are typical for Philippine waters and reflect 2026 market conditions. You can yacht charter Siargao for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Siargao

Most charters depart from General Luna, the main tourist hub on the island. The nearby port at Dapa serves as a jumping-off point for longer trips. Popular anchorages include Daku Island, Guyam Island, and Naked Island, all within a few nautical miles of the coast. The cluster of islets in the Sugba Lagoon area offers calm, sheltered waters ideal for beginners. For open-water sailing, the channel between Siargao and Bucas Grande Island provides steady winds and stunning scenery.

Which boat to rent

Your choice of vessel depends on your group size, budget, and desired experience. Below is a list of popular options available through local operators and on BoatHire24. One option worth a look is the Fairline listing.

  1. Local outrigger boat (bangka), Best for small groups of 4-6 guests exploring nearby islands; from 80 EUR for a half-day.
  2. Small motorboat (25-30 ft), Ideal for couples or families of up to 6, offering speed and flexibility; from 250 EUR for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran (35-45 ft), Spacious and stable, suitable for parties of 10-12 on full-day trips; from 600 EUR per day.
  4. Crewed yacht (Fairline or similar), A luxury option for up to 8 guests with professional crew; from 1,200 EUR per day. For an example of a premium vessel, see the Fairline listing on BoatHire24.
  5. Sailboat (30-40 ft), For a quieter, wind-powered experience; often chartered with a skipper for 400-700 EUR per day.

Costs and what they include

Rental prices in Siargao are generally all-inclusive for fuel and crew on longer charters. Short tours may exclude food and snorkelling gear.

  • Shared group tour (3-4 hours): from 40 EUR per person, includes guide and snacks.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from 250 EUR for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from 600 EUR for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from 1,200 EUR for up to 8 guests, including fuel and crew.
Book your charter early if you plan to visit Siargao in March or April; these months see a lull in the swell, making for calm crossings to the outer islands.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Siargao?

You do not need a licence to charter a boat with a skipper, but a valid international driving permit is required for self-drive rentals. Most visitors opt for a crewed charter, as the local waters have reefs and channels best navigated by an expert. The operator will confirm exact requirements, so always check before booking.

At a glance

  • Price band: 40 EUR shared tour to 1,200 EUR per day crewed yacht
  • Peak months: August, November to February (dry season)
  • Trip length: from 3 hours shared to 8 hours full-day
  • Typical guest capacity: 6-12 guests for private charters
  • Currency: all prices quoted in EUR (local payment may be in Philippine pesos)

Questions about boat rental in Siargao

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Self-drive rentals are possible for experienced boaters with an international driving permit, but most visitors prefer to hire a local skipper for safety and local knowledge.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip?

The dry season from August to February offers the most reliable weather, with calm seas and blue skies, though the surfing season brings bigger waves on the east coast.

Are there any hidden fees I should know about?

Always confirm what is included; some charters charge extra for fuel, food, or landing fees to certain islands, but many operators bundle these into the price.

Can I customise my itinerary on a private charter?

Yes, private charters allow you to choose your destinations, duration, and activities, from island hopping to snorkelling trips around the Sugba Lagoon.

To book a vessel for your Siargao adventure, browse options on boathire24.com, where you can also list your own boat and reach thousands of travellers worldwide. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Siargao or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Siargao and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
